external.idea: **Idea:** select 20% of the compounds most distant in descriptor space, train the model on the rest, and predict those compounds. This simulates real predictions for new, dissimilar compounds.

incremental.too_few_compounds: Too few compounds: number of compounds must exceed number of selected increments.
incremental.description:
This tool works with **existing numeric columns** in the table.

Two modes are available:

**1. OLS enabled**  
The program builds a linear model using ordinary least squares:

`Property = b0 + a1·X1 + a2·X2 + ... + an·Xn`

This is analogous to Excel's **LINEST** function.

**2. OLS disabled**  
The program does not build a model within this tool, but simply passes the selected numeric columns as custom descriptors to the main QSPR module.
incremental.no_candidates: No numeric columns suitable for custom descriptors were found in the dataset.
incremental.found_candidates_caption: The program automatically found candidate numeric columns. Below you can exclude auxiliary or unnecessary features.
incremental.used_features_info: Features to be used: {count} out of {total} found numeric columns.
incremental.calc_mnk_checkbox: Compute OLS model within this tool
incremental.calc_mnk_help: If enabled, the program will build a linear OLS model using the selected custom descriptors. If disabled, the selected columns will be passed as ready descriptors to the main QSPR module.
incremental.intercept_help_expander: ℹ️ How to interpret OLS contributions
incremental.mnk_disabled_info: OLS is disabled. When run, the selected numeric columns will be passed to the main QSPR module as custom descriptors.
incremental.calc_mnk_button: 🧮 Compute OLS model using selected descriptors
incremental.log_mnk_calculated: OLS model computed using custom descriptors: {n} features

incremental.log_transferred: Custom descriptors passed to QSPR: {n} features
incremental.success_transferred: Selected custom descriptors passed to the main QSPR module: {n_desc} features, {n_compounds} compounds.
incremental.error_transfer: Could not pass custom descriptors to QSPR: {error}
incremental.transfer_these_descriptors_button: ➡️ Use these descriptors in the main QSPR module

incremental.success_transferred_from_mnk: Selected custom descriptors passed to the main QSPR module.

spectra_desc.description:
This block converts selected IR- or Mass-spectra from `spectra_bank` into a numeric descriptor table for QSPR modelling.

Methodological logic:  
**spectrum → uniform grid → normalisation → spectral features → descriptor table**.
spectra_desc.method_mode_help: Vazhev mode is closest to the idea of using the entire spectrum as a numerical representation of structure. Compressed mode yields compact features. Custom mode gives full control.
spectra_desc.info_vazhev: Vazhev mode: uses a uniform spectral grid, strict phase selection, GRID descriptors and SVD components. Additionally adds MolWt / ExactMolWt as control sparring properties.
spectra_desc.info_compressed: Compressed mode: fewer raw spectral points, more emphasis on BAND/SVD features. Convenient for small datasets.
spectra_desc.info_custom: Custom mode: all grid, normalisation, and descriptor type parameters are set manually.
spectra_desc.phase_radio_label: Which spectra to use by phase / sample state
spectra_desc.phase_radio_help: For rigorous QSPR models it is better not to mix spectra of different phases. For Vazhev mode, gas-phase spectra are preferred.
spectra_desc.phase_manual_warning: Select at least one phase or change the spectrum selection mode.
spectra_desc.phase_any_warning: Mode 'Any active spectra' selected. Spectra of different phases may enter the descriptors. This is not recommended for rigorous QSPR.
spectra_desc.phase_prefer_info: A gas-phase spectrum will be selected if available. If no gas-phase spectrum exists, the program will take the next available spectrum by priority.
spectra_desc.phase_only_info: Only gas-phase spectra will be used. Compounds without a gas-phase spectrum will not appear in the spectral descriptor table.
spectra_desc.prefer_quantitative_help: Quantitative spectra can be used separately, but mixing them thoughtlessly with regular spectra is discouraged.
spectra_desc.invert_signal_help: For transmission spectra, minima correspond to absorption bands. For Mass, typically off.
spectra_desc.method_help_title: ℹ️ Methodology: spectrum as structural descriptor
spectra_desc.warning_select_descriptor: Select at least one type of spectral descriptor.
spectra_desc.sparring_checkbox: Add MolWt / ExactMolWt for future sparring control
spectra_desc.sparring_help: These columns are not spectral descriptors. They serve as control properties: later one can verify how well the spectral model recovers molecular weight.
spectra_desc.sparring_caption: Sparring control will be used in the next modelling stage: the model simultaneously predicts the target property and the control property MolWt / ExactMolWt.
spectra_desc.error_axis_min_max: Spectrum axis minimum must be less than maximum.
spectra_desc.success_calculation: Spectral descriptor calculation complete. Rows created: {count}.
spectra_desc.warning_empty_table: Calculation completed, but descriptor table is empty. Check for active spectra of the selected type, phase, source, and grid range.

spectra_report.warning_mixed_phases: Spectra of different phases/states entered the spectral descriptor table. This is not necessarily an error, but for rigorous QSPR it is better to use one phase or 'Gas only' mode.
spectra_report.svd_info: SVD components created: {created}. Total explained variance: {variance:.3f}
spectra_report.no_descriptors: Spectral descriptors not created. Possibly no active IR spectra for the compounds.
spectra_report.transfer_caption: Here only the calculated spectral descriptors are passed. Which sources to use in the model — molecular, spectral, or both — is selected below in the 'Descriptor source' section.
spectra_report.transfer_button: 📥 Assemble selected descriptor matrix for QSPR
spectra_report.matrix_ready: Spectral matrix connected: {rows} compounds × {cols} descriptors. Source: {source}.

bootstrap.min_compounds: At least 5 compounds are recommended for Bootstrap.
bootstrap.ui_description: **Bootstrap validation** repeatedly samples the training set with replacement and evaluates the model on out-of-bag objects.
bootstrap.ui_recommendation: Recommended first run: 100 iterations. Use 300–1000 iterations for final analysis.

y_randomization.conclusion_strong: model is statistically non‑random, strong confirmation
y_randomization.conclusion_moderate: model is likely non‑random, but confirmation is moderate
y_randomization.conclusion_not_confirmed: non‑randomness of model not confirmed by this test

repeated_holdout.min_compounds: At least 5 compounds are recommended for Repeated Hold-out.
repeated_holdout.conclusion_stable: model is stable to random train/test splits
repeated_holdout.conclusion_weak: model shows weak or unstable stability
repeated_holdout.conclusion_low: stability of model according to Repeated Hold-out is low
repeated_holdout.description:
**Repeated Hold-out / Monte-Carlo cross-validation** checks how stable the model is to different random train/test splits.

Unlike Y‑randomization, the property values `y` are not shuffled. The program repeatedly splits the original data into training and test sets, retrains the model, and computes average test metrics.
repeated_holdout.recommendation_caption: Recommended first run: 20–50 repeats. For reports or publications you may use 100–500 repeats.
repeated_holdout.summary_title: ### Summary of mean / std / min / max
repeated_holdout.distribution_title: ### Distribution of test metrics over repeats
repeated_holdout.hist_r2_title: Repeated Hold-out: distribution of Test R²
repeated_holdout.hist_rmse_title: Repeated Hold-out: distribution of Test RMSE

header.qspr: 🧪 QSPR (Quantitative Structure‑Property Relationship): building mathematical models to predict molecular characteristics from structural descriptors without synthesis.

upload.prompt: Select CSV or XLSX file for analysis
upload.xlsx_info: XLSX file will be read as an Excel table.
upload.unsupported_format: Only CSV and XLSX are supported.
upload.sample_alkanes_download: Download XLSX example: alkanes, boiling point (153.xlsx)

data_prep.header: 🧭 Data preparation and quality control module before modelling
data_prep.caption: Before modelling, additional procedures can be performed: find spectra, generate spectral descriptors, check structural consistency, and identify suspicious values.
data_prep.no_smiles_column: No column with molecular structures found. A SMILES or canonical_smiles column is required.
data_prep.smiles_tooltip: Select the column containing molecular structures in SMILES format.
data_prep.target_tooltip: Select the numeric column containing the values of the property to be modelled.
data_prep.standardization_info: This tool does not clean the dataset or remove rows. It brings SMILES to a uniform representation: checks structure, selects the main fragment, normalises the record, neutralises charges, generates canonical SMILES and InChIKey. Duplicates and conflicting records are only diagnosed; removal is performed by separate data quality tools.
data_prep.standardization_caption: Check and unify structures before descriptor calculation: salts/mixtures, main fragment, neutralisation, canonical SMILES, InChIKey, and duplicates.

standardization_ui.log_normalization: SMILES normalisation and canonicalisation: {initial} -> {final} rows without removal; duplicates detected: {duplicates}
standardization_ui.duplicates_caption: These rows are shown for diagnostic purposes only. The normalisation tool does not remove them from the working dataset.
standardization_ui.apply_button: ✅ Replace SMILES with canonical SMILES in working dataset
standardization_ui.error_no_smiles_col: SMILES column `{col}` not found in working dataset.
standardization_ui.data_source_note: Using normalised dataset: {count} rows. All original columns (including descriptors) preserved.
standardization_ui.apply_log: Applied SMILES normalisation and canonicalisation without removing rows: {rows} rows, {cols} columns.
standardization_ui.apply_success: Normalisation and canonicalisation applied. Canonical SMILES applied to working dataset. Row count unchanged; duplicates and conflicting records were not removed.

descriptor_settings.bank_caption: The first version of the bank works for the `default` profile. Only descriptors (without target property) are stored. Currently the bank is connected to xTB, morfeus, and DScribe.
descriptor_settings.xtb_limit_label: Limit number of molecules for xTB, 0 = all
descriptor_settings.morfeus_sasa_checkbox: SASA: solvent accessible surface area / volume
descriptor_settings.morfeus_optimize_checkbox: Optimise 3D geometry with UFF before morfeus
descriptor_settings.morfeus_limit_label: Limit molecules for morfeus test, 0 = all
descriptor_settings.morfeus_caption: SASA/Dispersion are usually faster. morfeus XTB may be noticeably slower. For the first test, keep 20 molecules.
descriptor_settings.dscribe_optimize_checkbox: Optimise 3D geometry with UFF before DScribe
descriptor_settings.dscribe_limit_label: Limit molecules for DScribe test, 0 = all
descriptor_settings.dscribe_caption: The first version of DScribe uses Coulomb Matrix eigenspectrum. The number of descriptors equals the maximum number of atoms. If a molecule has more atoms than this limit, the row will get status too_many_atoms.
descriptor_settings.spectral_not_ready_caption: Spectral descriptors become available after the ‘Assemble selected descriptor matrix for QSPR’ button in the spectral summary block.
descriptor_settings.warning_select_descriptor_type: Select at least one descriptor type.

descriptor_calc.error_xtb_not_available: xTB selected but xtb‑python not available in the current environment.
descriptor_calc.warning_xtb_bank_profile: The xTB descriptor bank is used only for the `default` profile: GFN2‑xTB, charge=0, uhf=0, accuracy=1.0, electronic temperature=300 K, max iterations=250, RDKit optimize=True. Current settings differ, so xTB will be recalculated.
descriptor_calc.xtb_bank_success: New xTB descriptors added to bank.
descriptor_calc.xtb_bank_error: Could not save xTB descriptors to bank: {error}
descriptor_calc.xtb_table_caption: Calculated quantum‑chemical xTB descriptors shown. Only rows with status `ok` enter modelling.
descriptor_calc.morfeus_not_available: morfeus selected but module unavailable. Import error: {error}
descriptor_calc.morfeus_bank_profile_warning: The morfeus descriptor bank is used only for the `default` profile: SASA=True, Dispersion=True, morfeus XTB=True, UFF optimize=True. Current settings differ, so morfeus will be recalculated.
descriptor_calc.morfeus_table_caption: 3D/steric/dispersion descriptors shown. Only rows with status `ok` enter modelling.
descriptor_calc.dscribe_not_available: DScribe selected but module unavailable. Import error: {error}
descriptor_calc.dscribe_bank_profile_warning: The DScribe descriptor bank is used only for the `default` profile: Coulomb Matrix eigenspectrum, UFF optimize=True, max_atoms=60. Current settings differ, so DScribe will be recalculated.
descriptor_calc.dscribe_bank_success: New DScribe descriptors added to bank.
descriptor_calc.dscribe_bank_error: Could not save DScribe descriptors to bank: {error}
descriptor_calc.dscribe_table_caption: DScribe Coulomb Matrix eigenspectrum descriptors shown. Only rows with status `ok` enter modelling.
descriptor_calc.spectral_not_found_warning: Spectral descriptors selected but spectral descriptor table not found. The matrix will be used without spectral descriptors.
descriptor_calc.meaning_table_status: calculated and included in current matrix
descriptor_calc.morfeus_bank_success: Morfeus descriptors were added to the bank.

desc_calc.pending_bundle_info: The assembled descriptor matrix is awaiting connection. Click ‘Connect assembled spectral matrix’ in the ‘Descriptor source’ block.
desc_calc.spectral_ready_info: Spectral descriptors calculated, but the matrix for QSPR is not yet assembled. Click ‘Assemble selected descriptor matrix for QSPR’.
desc_calc.calc_or_connect_info: Calculate or connect descriptors to proceed to modelling.

leakage_control.title: 🛡️ Data leakage control
leakage_control.success: No obvious data leakage indicators found in the current descriptor matrix.
leakage_control.warning_title: ⚠️ Potential leakage in current descriptor matrix
leakage_control.error_text: It is recommended to exclude suspicious features before training. Otherwise model metrics may be artificially inflated.
leakage_control.checkbox_label: I understand the risk and want to continue training with these features
leakage_control.failed: Could not perform data leakage control: {error}

mahalanobis.title: Potential multivariate outliers
mahalanobis.reason_text: Mahalanobis distance ({mahal:.3f}) exceeds the 95% threshold ({thresh:.3f}). This indicates an unusual combination of descriptors relative to other compounds.
mahalanobis.no_outliers: No potential multivariate outliers by Mahalanobis distance detected.
mahalanobis.insufficient_data: Insufficient data for multivariate outliers: need n > p + 10.
mahalanobis.interpret_expander: ℹ️ How to interpret these compounds
mahalanobis.outliers_table_empty: Potential multivariate outliers table is empty.
mahalanobis.structures_title: ⚛️ Structures of potential multivariate outliers by Mahalanobis distance

corr_table.section_title: ### Correlation of descriptors with target property

corr_heatmap.title: Correlations: property + non‑repeating top descriptors
corr_heatmap.caption: If the map is almost entirely red, the selected top descriptors are strongly positively correlated with each other and with the target property.

outliers_remove.checkbox_label: ✂️ Exclude target property outliers by IQR/z‑score from modelling
outliers_remove.help_text: Rows are not removed from the original working dataset. They are only excluded from the X/y matrix used for training models.

model_selection.kernel_help_title: ℹ️ About kernel‑based and similarity‑based methods
model_selection.genetic_help_title: ℹ️ About genetic and interpretable methods

model_params.mlp_hidden_layers_help: Example: 100,50 means two hidden layers: 100 and 50 neurons.
model_params.mlp_warning: MLP may overfit on small datasets. Always check Hold‑out, K‑Fold, or LOO, not only R² on the training set.
model_params.svr_info: SVR is a kernel‑based regression method. It works well for small‑ to medium‑sized QSPR datasets, especially when the relationship between descriptors and property is non‑linear.
model_params.gpr_info: GPR — Gaussian process. The method is useful because it can potentially provide not only predictions but also uncertainty estimates. In the current mode it is used as a regression model; confidence intervals can be added separately as a next step.
model_params.knn_info: KNN predicts the property by the k most similar compounds. It is a simple and transparent method, useful as a baseline model.
model_params.adaboost_info: AdaBoost is a simple boosting method that sequentially improves weak models. Works well on small datasets but is sensitive to outliers.
model_params.stacking_passthrough_label: Pass original descriptors to final model
model_params.stacking_info: Stacking combines available ensemble models; the final model is Ridge.
model_params.cart_info: CART — regression decision tree. The model is highly interpretable, but may overfit with large depth.
model_params.mars_info: MARS‑like Regression — an approximate version of MARS: expanded features are created, then Ridge regression is trained. This is not full Cubist/MARS, but a simple interpretable step in that direction.
model_params.et_max_features_label: Number of features for split search
model_params.et_info: Extra Trees — extremely randomised trees: even higher randomness level than Random Forest. Often gives good quality on large datasets; may perform worse on small sets.
model_params.gep_info_repeated: GEP Symbolic Regression — evolutionary search for a short formula linking descriptors to the property. The result is interpretable, but calculation may be slow.
model_params.gep_info: GEP Symbolic Regression — evolutionary search for a short formula linking descriptors to the property. The result is interpretable, but calculation may be slow.
model_params.hgb_info: Histogram Gradient Boosting is efficient for medium and large tabular datasets.

model_encyclopedia.title: ## 📚 Model encyclopedia
model_encyclopedia.hyperparams_desc: Settings that can be adjusted in the interface to improve the model.

auto_select.expander_title: ⚖️ Automatic descriptor selection and hyperparameter optimisation
auto_select.method_fast: Fast selection: constant + correlated + top‑k by F‑score
auto_select.max_features_label: How many descriptors to keep finally
auto_select.corr_caption: If two descriptors are strongly correlated, the program keeps the one more correlated with the target property. This reduces multicollinearity and the risk of spurious models.
auto_select.help_title: ℹ️ How to choose the selection mode?

auto_results.heatmap_min_descriptors: At least 2 selected descriptors are required for the heatmap.
auto_results.heatmap_not_found: Not enough selected descriptors found in the current X matrix to build the heatmap.
auto_results.heatmap_insufficient_cols: Insufficient numeric columns for correlation heatmap.
auto_results.heatmap_plot_title: Correlations of first selected descriptors and property {col}
auto_results.heatmap_caption: The map shows the first 15 finally selected descriptors after auto‑selection and the target property. If strong correlations remain among selected descriptors, you can decrease the |r| threshold or reduce the number of finally selected descriptors.
auto_results.heatmap_error: Could not build heatmap of selected descriptors: {error}

applicability_domain.expander_help_title: ℹ️ Applicability Domain and Williams Plot
applicability_domain.mol_grid_high_residual: ⚛️ Structures of compounds with large residuals
applicability_domain.mol_grid_critical: ⚛️ Structures of critical Williams Plot compounds
applicability_domain.success_no_critical: No critical Williams Plot compounds found.
applicability_domain.warning_outside_ad: Compounds outside the model applicability domain found: {count}.
applicability_domain.success_all_in_ad: All training compounds are within the applicability domain by leverage.
applicability_domain.mol_grid_outside_ad: ⚛️ Structures of compounds outside the model applicability domain

save_model.verified_caption: The package includes the model, descriptors, training set, and validation status.
save_model.validation_required: The final package can be saved after Hold-out, K-Fold, LOO, or external validation.
save_model.button: 💾 Save analytical model as full package
save_model.log: Analytical model saved as full package

model_comparison.description: This block does not merely compare validations of one already chosen model. It automatically trains a set of candidate models on the same descriptors, computes Hold‑out / K‑Fold / LOO, and selects the most stable model. The main criterion is not training R² but cross‑validation and prediction errors.
model_comparison.no_models_warning: List of available models not found.
model_comparison.candidates_label: Candidate models for automatic training and comparison
model_comparison.candidates_help: Select several algorithms. Heavy models like GEP, MLP, CatBoost, XGBoost may take longer.
model_comparison.run_loo_help: LOO may be slow, especially with many models.
model_comparison.force_retrain_help: If disabled, already trained models and already computed checks will be reused.
model_comparison.recommendation_caption: Recommended first run: MLR, PLS, Ridge, LASSO, Elastic Net, SVR, KNN, Random Forest, CART, MARS‑like. LOO is better included after initial screening to avoid long waits.
model_comparison.montecarlo_top_checkbox: 🎉 Monte‑Carlo CV only for top models
model_comparison.bootstrap_top_checkbox: 🏅 Bootstrap CV only for top models
model_comparison.top_n_label: How many top models to check additionally
model_comparison.run_button: 🏆 Train, compare models and select the best
model_comparison.warning_select_models: Select at least one model for comparison.
model_comparison.spinner_training: Automatic training and validation of candidate models...
model_comparison.progress_stage1: Stage 1: training all models without LOO...
model_comparison.top_models_info: Additional checks launched only for top‑{n} models: {models}.
model_comparison.progress_stage2: Stage 2: LOO only for best models...
model_comparison.success_best_model: Best model by combined ranking: {model}. Interface will switch to it.
model_comparison.no_comparison_info: Click ‘Train, compare models and select the best’. The program will then train the selected algorithms, compute validations, and build the ranking.

consensus.description: Combines predictions from several top models to improve accuracy and estimate uncertainty. Trained models with their scalers are used.
consensus.top_n_label: Number of top models for consensus
consensus.warning_no_models_after_filter: No models satisfy the R² threshold. Try lowering the threshold.
consensus.warning_need_two_models: At least 2 models are required for consensus.
consensus.error_loading_models: Could not load models. Ensure they are trained.
consensus.info_train_first: First train at least one model.

report.description: The report is generated based on the stages already completed: data, descriptors, model, validation, Applicability Domain, SAOD, structural filter, spectra, model comparison, and prognostic model.
report.info_no_descriptors: First calculate or connect descriptors. Then the report will become available.
report.unavailable_auto_appear: These sections will appear automatically after the corresponding stages are performed:
report.section_saod_structures: 🧪 Structures of compounds requiring manual SAOD review
report.descriptor_filtered: Filtered descriptors actually used by the trained model
report.descriptor_all: All calculated descriptors in the current matrix
report.distribution_boxplot_section_title: Distribution and boxplot of target property
report.coef_not_extracted: Coefficients / importances not extracted for this model.
report.section_charts_comparison: Comparison of experimental and predicted values
report.ad_error_comment: Could not include Applicability Domain in the report: {error}
report.quality_excellent: Model describes the training set well.
report.quality_good: Model describes the training set moderately.
report.quality_poor: Description of the training set is weak.
report.quality_outliers: Compounds with elevated prediction error found: {count}.
report.quality_outliers_structures_section: Structures of compounds with large prediction errors
report.consensus_quality_comment: Consensus prediction built from {n_models} models: {models}. Mean standard deviation of predictions: {mean_std:.3f}.
report.quality_default_comment: Model quality should be assessed by the combined set of training metrics, cross‑validation, prediction errors, and Applicability Domain.
report.success_generated: Report on current QSPR analysis generated.
report.error_generation: Error generating report on current QSPR analysis: {error}
report.final_statistics_error: Final statistics were not added to the report: {error}

methodology.copy_instruction: Select the text and copy (Ctrl+C / Cmd+C).
methodology.no_history_info: Click ‘Generate methodology text’ to create the first version.

report_full.caption: Creates a structured draft report with the available data from the current analysis: dataset, descriptors, model, validation, AD, and diagnostics. Sections with unavailable data are marked as not filled.
report_full.no_history_info: No draft reports have been generated yet.

model_catalog.no_matches: No models match the selected criteria. The full catalog is shown.

y_rand.ui_description: **Y-randomization** tests whether model performance could have arisen by chance by repeatedly shuffling the target values and retraining the model.
y_rand.ui_loo_warning: LOO with many permutations may take a long time.
y_rand.ui_recommendation: Use at least 100 permutations; 500–1000 are recommended for publication-level analysis.

feature_importance.title: 📌 Descriptor importance
feature_importance.causality_warning: Importance shows how the model uses a descriptor; it does not prove a causal chemical mechanism.
feature_importance.native_warning: Tree-based native importance may be biased. Compare it with permutation importance.
feature_importance.no_native: This model exposes neither coefficients nor native feature importance.
feature_importance.holdout_source: Permutation importance will be calculated on the independent hold-out set.
feature_importance.training_source_warning: Hold-out has not been run. The result will use training data and may be optimistic.
feature_importance.negative_info: {count} descriptors have negative mean importance. This may indicate noise or an unstable estimate.
feature_importance.correlated_warning: Correlated descriptors can replace one another. Grouped permutation estimates their joint contribution.
feature_importance.shap_unavailable: The SHAP package is not installed.
feature_importance.shap_caption: TreeExplainer is used for tree models; other models may require substantially more computation.
feature_importance.summary_empty: Calculate at least one available importance method first.
feature_importance.summary_caption: The summary combines available importance estimates. Agreement between several methods increases confidence in the ranking.
feature_importance.validation_required: Run Hold-out, K-Fold, LOO, or external validation first.
feature_importance.validated_training_source: Validation is complete. Without Hold-out, permutation importance is calculated on the training matrix and must be interpreted cautiously.

diagnostics.residual_descriptor_caption: Residual is calculated as experimental − predicted. A positive residual means the model underpredicted; a negative residual means it overpredicted.
diagnostics.residual_descriptor_unavailable: Not enough data to plot residuals against descriptors.
diagnostics.residual_prediction_error: Could not calculate predictions for residual diagnostics: {error}
diagnostics.residual_descriptor_not_numeric: The selected descriptor does not have enough numeric values.
diagnostics.residual_direction_under: as the descriptor increases, the model more often underpredicts
diagnostics.residual_direction_over: as the descriptor increases, the model more often overpredicts
diagnostics.residual_top_title: Top-10 descriptors most associated with error
diagnostics.residual_top_empty: No descriptor had an estimable relationship with residuals.

error_analysis.title: 🔎 Where does the model fail?
error_analysis.description: This analysis identifies chemical groups and regions of structural space with increased or systematic prediction error.
error_analysis.no_model: Train a model and run at least one validation method first.
error_analysis.training_warning: Error source: {source}. This is preliminary because training-set errors may be underestimated.
error_analysis.independent_source: Error source: {source}. Independent predictions are being used.
error_analysis.bootstrap_repeats: Bootstrap repeats for the MAE interval
error_analysis.length_mismatch: SMILES, experimental, and predicted arrays have different lengths.
error_analysis.overlap_warning: Groups overlap: a compound may simultaneously be aromatic, halogenated, and nitrogen-containing. Group counts therefore do not sum to the dataset size.
error_analysis.no_groups: No groups meet the selected minimum size.
error_analysis.cluster_explanation: Clusters are generated automatically from Morgan fingerprints and Tanimoto similarity. They may reveal weak structural regions that were not predefined.
error_analysis.no_clusters: No sufficiently large clusters were found at the selected similarity threshold.
error_analysis.select_group: Select a group to inspect compounds
error_analysis.description_structural: The primary analysis uses structural series, scaffolds, and normalized substitution patterns. General chemical features and fingerprint clusters remain secondary diagnostics.
error_analysis.min_series_size: Minimum series size for a reliable conclusion
error_analysis.no_effect_pairs: No pairs with a suitable reference series were found.
error_analysis.secondary_groups_note: These overlapping groups are secondary diagnostics and do not replace structural-series analysis.

prediction_page.full_uncertainty_help: Adds a calibrated prediction interval, query bootstrap, local analogue error, AD signals, and reliability status.
prediction_page.settings_note: The calibrated conformal interval is the prediction interval. Consensus std, bootstrap std, and GPR std are shown separately as spread diagnostics.
prediction_page.train_prog_first: First train a prognostic model in the block above.
prediction_page.standalone_description: Apply an already trained prognostic model to new compounds. You can load a saved `.pkl` model package, draw a molecule, or upload a table with SMILES / descriptors.
prediction_page.load_or_train_first: First load a saved model package or train a prognostic model in the QSPR modelling section.
prediction_page.new_compound_description: Apply the trained prognostic model to new compounds. If the model was trained on molecular descriptors, upload a file with SMILES. If it was trained on custom, spectral, or combined descriptors, upload a table with the same descriptor columns.
prediction_page.draw_molecule_expander: ✏️ Draw a molecule and predict the property
prediction_page.molecular_editor_caption: Draw a molecule, obtain SMILES, and calculate the prediction without uploading a separate file.
prediction_page.ketcher_missing: The streamlit-ketcher package is not installed. Install it with: pip install streamlit-ketcher
prediction_page.smiles_input_help: You can paste SMILES manually or use SMILES from the editor.
prediction_page.empty_smiles_error: First draw a molecule or enter SMILES.
prediction_page.invalid_smiles_error: RDKit could not parse the SMILES. Check the structure.
prediction_page.spectral_model_info: The current model contains spectral descriptors. The app will first check the local spectra_bank, then try to find a spectrum online. If no spectrum is found, SMILES prediction cannot be performed.
prediction_page.spectral_smiles_warning: The current model contains spectral descriptors. From SMILES, only structural/quantum-chemical features can be calculated; the spectrum cannot be reconstructed. Use the mode “By ready descriptors from file”.
prediction_page.descriptor_calc_info: Descriptors matching the current model will be calculated for prediction: `{source}`. Sets: {parts}. Required features: {count}.
prediction_page.smiles_descriptor_info: Descriptors matching the model will be calculated from SMILES: `{source}`. Sets: {parts}. Required features: {count}.
prediction_page.drawn_spinner: Calculating model descriptors and prediction for the drawn molecule...
prediction_page.drawn_prediction_error: Could not predict the drawn molecule: {error}
prediction_page.smiles_column: SMILES column in the new file
prediction_page.ready_descriptor_caption: The table must contain descriptor columns with the same names that were used to train the prognostic model.
prediction_page.no_required_descriptors: The file does not contain any required descriptor.
prediction_page.model_has_spectral_warning: This model contains spectral descriptors. They cannot be reconstructed from one drawn structure or SMILES: use prediction from a file with ready descriptors.
prediction_page.model_needs_3d_warning: This model requires 3D/quantum-chemical descriptors (xTB/morfeus/DScribe). Prediction from a drawn molecule is possible only if the corresponding calculation modules are available and can process the structure.
prediction_page.model_smiles_ready: This model is suitable for prediction from SMILES / a drawn molecule.
prediction_page.spectral_model_draw_block: The current model contains spectral descriptors. Spectral features cannot be reconstructed from one structure/SMILES, so prediction for a drawn molecule is unavailable for this model.
prediction_page.spectral_model_file_advice: Choose “By ready descriptors from file” and upload a table with the same descriptor columns that were used in the saved model.
prediction_page.scenario_known_model: I know which model to use
prediction_page.capability_intro: ### What can be predicted for this molecule?
prediction_page.capability_no_models: No saved prognostic models were found.
prediction_page.capability_no_runnable_models: No found model can run prediction from SMILES. Use manual mode or a ready-descriptor file.
prediction_page.capability_tool_unavailable: The chemical classification tool is unavailable.
prediction_page.capability_reason_spectral_descriptors: the model requires spectral descriptors that cannot be reconstructed from SMILES
prediction_page.capability_reason_requires_3d: the model requires 3D/quantum-chemical descriptors; calculation may be slow or unavailable
prediction_page.capability_reason_smiles_runnable: descriptors can be calculated from SMILES
prediction_page.spectral_ad_interp_reliable: Molecular AD, Spectral AD, and Combined AD are inside the training domain: the prediction is more reliable.
prediction_page.spectral_ad_interp_spectral_out: The structure is similar to training compounds, but the spectral profile is outside the training domain.
prediction_page.spectral_ad_interp_molecular_out: The spectral profile is similar to training compounds, but the structure is outside the molecular domain.
prediction_page.spectral_ad_interp_combined_out: The compound is outside the combined model domain: treat the final prediction cautiously.
prediction_page.spectral_ad_interp_not_calculated: Separate molecular/spectral AD was calculated only partially or is unavailable for this model.

prediction_uncertainty.description: Predicts new SMILES or descriptor tables and separately calculates a calibrated conformal interval, query bootstrap, local analogue error, model disagreement, GPR uncertainty, and applicability-domain signals.
prediction_uncertainty.train_first: First train at least one model.
prediction_uncertainty.too_few_training_objects: At least three training compounds are required for uncertainty estimation.
prediction_uncertainty.standalone_description: Load a saved model package and predict new compounds with complete uncertainty and applicability-domain assessment.
prediction_uncertainty.trusted_package_warning: Only load model files created by you or a trusted source: joblib/pickle files can execute Python code when opened.
prediction_uncertainty.package_first: First load a saved model package.
prediction_uncertainty.package_error: Could not open the model package: {error}
prediction_uncertainty.old_package: The package lacks data required for complete uncertainty: {fields}. Save the model again with the updated Augur QSPR version.
prediction_uncertainty.use_consensus: Use all compatible trained models for consensus
prediction_uncertainty.file_input: CSV/XLSX containing SMILES or all model descriptors
prediction_uncertainty.no_query: Enter SMILES or upload a table.
prediction_uncertainty.spinner: Calculating descriptors, predictions, intervals, and applicability domain...
prediction_uncertainty.interval_note: Conformal lower/upper is the calibrated prediction interval. Consensus std, bootstrap std, and GPR std are separate diagnostics and do not replace it.
prediction_uncertainty.error_no_descriptors: The trained model descriptor list was not found.
prediction_uncertainty.error_no_numeric_query: The uploaded table has no rows with a complete numeric descriptor set.
prediction_uncertainty.error_query_columns: The table must contain a SMILES column or every descriptor used by the trained model.
prediction_uncertainty.log_done: Complete uncertainty calculated with {model} for {count} compounds.
prediction_uncertainty.full_uncertainty_help: Adds a calibrated prediction interval, query bootstrap, local analogue error, AD signals, and reliability status.
prediction_uncertainty.settings_note: The calibrated conformal interval is the prediction interval. Consensus std, bootstrap std, and GPR std are shown separately as spread diagnostics.
prediction_uncertainty.train_prog_first: First train a prognostic model in the block above.

loo_guard.skip_general: LOO skipped: {n} compounds. In the cloud version, the LOO limit is {limit}; use K-Fold/hold-out or set AUGUR_QSPR_LOO_MAX_SAMPLES.
loo_guard.skip_heavy: LOO skipped for heavy model {model}: {n} compounds. In the cloud version, the limit for these models is {limit}; this prevents Streamlit Cloud from hanging and restarting.

final_stats.unavailable_comment: This block has not been calculated yet or there is not enough data.
final_stats.interpret_skew_asym: The target property distribution is noticeably asymmetric.
final_stats.interpret_skew_sym: The target property distribution has no strong asymmetry.
final_stats.interpret_range_wide: The property range is wide: check extreme values.
final_stats.interpret_not_enough: Not enough numeric values for interpretation.
final_stats.multicol_limited: Only the first {n} descriptors were checked to keep the interface responsive.
final_stats.quality_weak: Weak fit; the model needs improvement.
final_stats.descriptor_table_title: Descriptors with missing values and zeros
final_stats.correlation_table_title: Descriptors most correlated with the target property
